evals.name: ancilla-not-clean-debug
evals.prompt: My reversible multiplier produces the correct product, but when I drop it into a bigger algorithm the results get corrupted — and I traced it to some ancilla qubits not coming back to |0> at the end. How do I track down where the uncomputation is going wrong?

evals.name: validate-adder-for-shor
evals.prompt: I've written a reversible modular adder and it passes my test: I feed in computational-basis states and check the output bits are right. But I'm about to use it inside Shor's algorithm and I'm nervous. How should I actually validate that it's correct?

evals.name: designed-truncation-vs-bug
evals.prompt: My circuit deliberately truncates some carry bits to save qubits, so I expect it to give wrong answers on a small fraction of inputs. I'm seeing about 0.3% of random inputs fail. How do I tell whether that's just my intended truncation or an actual bug I introduced?
